word_list.pronunciation: lˈɪɾəl dɹˈɑːps mˌeɪk ðə mˈaɪɾi ˈoʊʃən

senses.definition: used to emphasize that small actions or contributions can lead to significant results over time

examples.x: "Saving money can be difficult, but little drops make the ocean, and small savings can add up over time," said the financial advisor during the consultation.

word_list.word: many a little makes a mickle
word_list.pronunciation: mˈɛni ɐ lˈɪɾəl mˌeɪks ɐ mˈɪkəl

senses.definition: used to suggest that even small or seemingly insignificant contributions or actions can add up over time and make a significant difference

examples.x: "In learning a new skill, consistent practice and small improvements can lead to mastery, as many a little makes a mickle," said the instructor during the class.

word_list.word: a journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ dʒˈɜːni əvə θˈaʊzənd mˈaɪlz bɪɡˈɪnz wɪð ɐ sˈɪŋɡəl stˈɛp

senses.definition: used to imply that even the most daunting tasks can be accomplished by taking small steps towards the goal, starting with a single action or decision

word_list.word: a stern chase is a long chase
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ stˈɜːn tʃˈeɪs ɪz ɐ lˈɑːŋ tʃˈeɪs

senses.definition: used to suggest that one needs to be persistent and determined to achieve what one wants, especially when starting from a disadvantaged position

examples.x: I know you're struggling to catch up with your studies, but remember that a stern chase is a long chase, so keep working hard and you'll get there eventually

word_list.word: after victory, tighten your helmet chord
word_list.pronunciation: ˈæftɚ vˈɪktɚɹi tˈaɪʔn̩ jʊɹ hˈɛlmɪt kˈɔːɹd

senses.definition: used to advise one to stay focused and prepared even after achieving success, as new challenges and obstacles may arise

word_list.pronunciation: kˈɑːnstənt dɹˈɑːpɪŋ ɔːɹ dɹˈɪpɪŋ wˈɛɹz ɐwˈeɪ ɐ ɔːɹ ðə stˈoʊn

senses.definition: used to imply that persistent and continuous effort, no matter how small, can eventually lead to significant change or achievement

examples.x: The athlete who trains regularly and consistently knows that constant dropping wears away a stone and is more likely to perform well in competition.

word_list.word: fair and softly goes far in a day
word_list.pronunciation: fˈɛɹ ænd sˈɔftli ɡoʊz fˈɑːɹ ɪn ɐ dˈeɪ

senses.definition: used to highlight the idea that consistent and steady progress can lead to significant accomplishments over time

examples.x: When training for a marathon, it's important to pace yourself and build up your endurance gradually, as fair and softly goes far in a day.

word_list.pronunciation: fˈeɪk ɪt tˈɪl ɔːɹ ʌntˈɪl juː fˈaɪnəli ɔːɹ mˈeɪk ɪt

senses.definition: used to suggest that by pretending to possess desired qualities or abilities, one can eventually develop those qualities or abilities for real

examples.x: When trying to overcome self-doubt and build self-confidence, it can be helpful to fake it till you make it, by acting as if you are confident and capable, even if you don't feel that way yet.

word_list.word: genius is an infinite capacity for taking pains
word_list.pronunciation: dʒˈiːnɪəs ɪz ɐn ˈɪnfɪnət kəpˈæsɪɾi fɔːɹ tˈeɪkɪŋ pˈeɪnz

senses.definition: used to emphasize that true genius is not solely dependent on natural talent, but also on the ability to consistently put in effort and hard work to achieve excellence

examples.x: Michelangelo's sculptures and paintings are a testament to the fact that genius is an infinite capacity for taking pains.

word_list.word: if at first you do not succeed, try, try again
word_list.pronunciation: ɪf æt fˈɜːst juː duːnˌɑːt səksˈiːd tɹˈaɪ tɹˈaɪ ɐɡˈɛn

senses.definition: used to encourage individuals to continue trying even after experiencing failure or setbacks, with the belief that persistence and resilience are key to achieving success

word_list.pronunciation: sˈiːk ænd juː ɔːɹ jiː ʃˌæl fˈaɪnd

senses.definition: used to imply that by actively searching for something, one can eventually discover what they are looking for, emphasizing the importance of being persistent and proactive in pursuing one's goals or desires

examples.x: The detective searched tirelessly for clues, believing that seek and you shall find and eventually solved the case.

word_list.word: slow and steady wins the race
word_list.pronunciation: slˈoʊ ænd stˈɛdi wˈɪnz ðə ɹˈeɪs

senses.definition: used to imply that consistent and persistent effort, even if it appears slow, is more likely to lead to success in a sustainable and efficient way

examples.x: The gardener tended to their plants with consistent care and attention, trusting that slow and steady wins the race, and eventually grew a beautiful garden

word_list.word: the third time pays for all
word_list.pronunciation: ðə θˈɜːd tˈaɪm pˈeɪz fɔːɹ ˈɔːl

senses.definition: used to encourage individuals to not give up after initial failures, and to keep trying until they achieve success

examples.x: The entrepreneur's first two business ventures failed, but he didn't give up and started a third business with the idea that the third time pays for all, which became a success.

word_list.word: the used key is always bright
word_list.pronunciation: ðə jˈuːzd kˈiː ɪz ˈɔːlweɪz bɹˈaɪt

senses.definition: said to suggest that through frequent use, something, such as a skill or object, can remain in good condition and work effectively for a long time

examples.x: The musician was able to play the difficult piece with ease because she had practiced it many times, and the used key is always bright.

senses.definition: used to imply that if something is not used or practiced, it will be forgotten or taken away

examples.x: If you don’t practice speaking French, you’ll forget it — use it or lose it.
Don’t stop learning new things — use it or lose it applies to your brain as well.
She was told to keep exercising regularly because use it or lose it applies to joint mobility too.
Use it or lose it — I was advised to keep practicing my writing skills.
If you stop using those skills, you might forget them — it’s true, use it or lose it.

senses.definition: used to suggest that when we develop a habit or routine of doing something repeatedly, it becomes easier and more effortless over time

examples.x: At first, running a mile every day may seem difficult, but as you continue to do it, it becomes easier - custom makes all things easy.

word_list.word: dogs bark, but the caravan goes on
word_list.pronunciation: dˈɑːɡz bˈɑːɹk bˌʌt ðə kˈæɹɐvˌæn ɡoʊz ˈɑːn

senses.definition: used to imply that one should not be discouraged by negative influences or setbacks and should remain focused on their goals and continue to move forward
used to emphasize the idea that small, continuous actions or efforts can eventually lead to significant achievements or outcomes

examples.x: Learning a new language can be a daunting task, but by practicing a little every day, you'll make steady progress, and eventually, little strokes fell great oaks, and you'll be fluent.

senses.definition: used to imply that regular and persistent practice of a skill or activity is necessary in order to become proficient or skilled at it

examples.x: When I started learning how to cook, my grandma told me that practice makes perfect, and encouraged me to keep trying different recipes.
